sections.content: The board changed after RIGHT. Let me compare the before and after frames to see what changed - specifically the white marker positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after to see what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

# Get white node positions in both frames
def get_white_positions(frame):
    seg = frame.segmentation
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'W':
            print(f"  W node: boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("Before RIGHT:")
get_white_positions(before)
print("\nAfter RIGHT:")
get_white_positions(after)

# Also check the ascii difference around the marker area
before_lines = before.ascii.split('\n')
after_lines = after.ascii.split('\n')
for i in range(46, 62):
    if before_lines[i] != after_lines[i]:
        print(f"\nRow {i} changed:")
        print(f"  Before: {before_lines[i]}")
        print(f"  After:  {after_lines[i]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me find all 5 symbol positions in the strips
# Strip spans from col 14 to col 48, with 5 symbols
# Each symbol seems to be about 7 columns wide
# Let me check the exact symbol boundaries

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
row41 = ascii_lines[41]  # Sky blue strip content row
row52 = ascii_lines[52]  # Pink strip content row

# Find symbol boundaries by looking for transitions
# The strip background is S (sky blue) for top, P (pink) for bottom
# Symbols are B (black)

# For sky blue strip, find B regions
print("Sky blue strip B regions (row 41):")
in_symbol = False
start = None
for j, c in enumerate(row41):
    if c == 'B' and not in_symbol:
        start = j
        in_symbol = True
    elif c != 'B' and in_symbol:
        print(f"  Symbol from col {start} to {j-1}")
        in_symbol = False
if in_symbol:
    print(f"  Symbol from col {start} to {len(row41)-1}")

# Let me also check the white marker centers
print("\nWhite marker centers after 1 RIGHT:")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  cols: min={min(cols)}, max={max(cols)}, center~{(min(cols)+max(cols))//2}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
